title: Tutor demo fallback transparency
prompt: Improve the tutor panel so demo-mode fallback is transparent, partial answers are preserved after a forced stream failure, and grounding details remain inspectable for the active topic.

scale.1: Fails the dimension or has no usable evidence.
scale.2: Has major gaps, regressions, or mostly claim-based evidence.
scale.3: Acceptable but incomplete, noisy, or only partially evidenced.
scale.4: Strong result with minor gaps or limited missing evidence.
scale.5: Excellent result with complete behavior and strong evidence.

criticalRequirements: Demo-mode fallback must be visible and understandable to the user.
Partial tutor answers must be preserved after a forced stream failure.
Grounding details for the active topic must remain inspectable.
Live/demo boundaries must not be blurred or misrepresented.

dimensions.description: How completely the final workspace improves tutor fallback transparency, partial-answer preservation, and grounding inspectability.
Whether stream-failure paths, demo fallback, preserved partial content, grounding details, tests, and typecheck behave correctly.
Maintainability, scope control, fit with existing tutor state flow, resilience of async/error handling, and focused tests.
Quality of research, planning, contract specificity, repair behavior, and whether the harness reasoned about streaming/runtime edge cases.
Whether verdicts are grounded in objective checks and concrete forced-failure or runtime evidence rather than agent assertions.

scoringNotes: Do not change this rubric based on run-generated eval criteria or contracts.
Evidence for the forced stream-failure path is especially important.
Unrelated rewrites outside the tutor flow should reduce implementationQuality.
